title: CLT Employee Total Cost Calculator
meta_description: Calculate the true cost of a CLT employee for your company in Brazil. Includes Employer INSS, RAT, FGTS, and mandatory 13th salary provisions.

hero.title: True Employee Cost Calculator
hero.subtitle: Discover the exact accounting cost of a Brazilian CLT employee with all taxes, benefits, and provisions (2026 updated tables).

formula: True Cost = Salary + Employer INSS (20%) + RAT + Third Parties (5.8%) + FGTS (8%) + Accrued Vacation/13th + Benefits
formula_explanation: This breakdown assumes standard tax regimes (Lucro Real/Presumido). It calculates 20% Employer INSS, RAT adjusted by the FAP accident multiplier, 5.8% for Third-Parties, and 8% FGTS. It also provisions an equivalent monthly fraction for the 13th salary and Vacations (+1/3 bonus).
example: A Brazilian employee holding a R$3,000 base salary will cost the company nearly R$5,100 per month from a cash-flow perspective after all social contributions and provisions are fully accounted for.

faq.question: What is the cost multiplier?
faq.answer: It's the ratio between the total employer cost and the base gross salary (e.g., 1.7x). Usually, hiring in Brazil's standard tax regimes represents an extra 70% to 90% overhead.

faq.question: Does this calculation apply to Simples Nacional SMEs?
faq.answer: No. Companies enrolled in 'Simples Nacional' pay their Social Security bundled within their main DAS tax bill and are exempt from the 20% INSS and 5.8% Third-Party levies (except specific Annex IV activities).

faq.question: Why provision Vacation and 13th Salary?
faq.answer: Even though they are paid annually, accounting principles dictate accruing their cost divisionally (1/12th) each worked month to prevent unexpected cashflow deficits.
